@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9a80
+project: local-review
+title: 'feat(llama-agent): session fork over ACP — new session seeded from a parent session''s state'
+---
+## What
+
+Foundation for prefix-session reuse in the review fleet (companion task orchestrates it). Add the ability to create a new ACP session that starts from an existing session's conversation + cached KV state, so a continuation decodes strictly FORWARD from the parent's saved position — zero KV rollback.
+
+Why: the cross-session LCP donor cache exists (`find_best_prefix_match`/`prepare_streaming_kv_cache` in crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs) but on the hybrid attention+recurrent Qwen, trimming a donor's KV back to the LCP fails (`clear_kv_cache_seq` → `Ok(false)`, snapshot-ring limit) and falls back to cold prefill. Evidence from ../calcutron/.sah/mcp.50099.log (2026-06-12): 406 donor matches, only 158 successful reuses, **404 trim fallbacks → full ~15k-token cold prefills**. A fork whose parent state is a strict prefix of the new prompt never needs rollback, so it sidesteps the limitation entirely.
+
+Design (verify protocol specifics against the agent-client-protocol crate before implementing):
+
+- [ ] ACP surface: a fork request via the protocol's extension mechanism — either an extension method (`session/fork` with `{parentSessionId}`) or `session/new` carrying `_meta: {forkFrom: <session-id>}` — whichever the agent-client-protocol crate's extensibility supports cleanly (we control both ends: the validators pool client and llama-agent's ACP server in crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs). Follow how session_resume.rs registered `session/resume`/`session/load` as precedent.
+- [ ] Agent side: fork clones the parent's Session (message history, config) under a fresh session id, and makes the parent's SessionStateCache entry available to the child with NO byte copy — share the state blob (e.g. `Arc<[u8]>`/`Arc<Vec<u8>>` in `CachedSession`) and register the child id with the parent's prompt-token fingerprint. The 2GB cache budget must count shared bytes once, not per fork.
+- [ ] First prompt on a forked session must hit the strict-prefix restore path: donor tokens are a prefix of the new rendered prompt → trim offset == donor length → no rollback (`clear_kv_cache_seq` of an empty range must not fail). Confirm the rendered chat-template tokens of [parent history + new user turn] really do extend the parent's saved tokens byte-for-byte — if the template re-renders history differently than the live turn rendered it (the session_record round-trip invariants are precedent), THAT is the bug to fix first.
+- [ ] Forked sessions are independent: child generation must not mutate the parent's cache entry (copy-on-write at save time — child's end-of-turn save inserts its own entry).
+- [ ] Errors: fork of unknown/evicted-state parent returns a clean error (fleet falls back to a plain new_session + full prompt).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Client can prime a session with a prompt turn, fork it N times, prompt each fork with different continuations, and every fork's first decode reuses the parent's full saved token count (observable via the "streaming reusing N cached tokens" log line) with zero "KV trim … returned false" fallbacks.
+- [ ] Parent remains usable after forks; forks are isolated from each other.
+- [ ] Cache byte accounting counts the shared parent blob once.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] llama-agent unit/integration tests with the scripted/fake generation seam (<10s, no real model): fork clones history + aliases state; first forked prompt computes strict-prefix offset (no rollback path taken); parent unaffected; unknown-parent fork errors cleanly; byte-accounting test for shared blobs.
+- [ ] One real-model integration test (allowed slow, integration lane): prime → fork ×2 → both forks' prompts log full-prefix reuse and produce sane replies on the Qwen test model — proving the hybrid-model no-rollback property end-to-end.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p llama-agent` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] Client can prime a session with a prompt turn, fork it N times, prompt each fork with different continuations, and every fork's first decode reuses the parent's full saved token count (observable via the "streaming reusing N cached tokens" log line) with zero "KV trim … returned false" fallbacks.
-- [ ] Parent remains usable after forks; forks are isolated from each other.
-- [ ] Cache byte accounting counts the shared parent blob once.

+- [ ] A fork whose parent state is missing reports it distinguishably (response field/error), never a silent cold prefill.
+- [ ] Pinned entries survive cache pressure that evicts unpinned ones; parent remains usable after forks; forks isolated; shared blob bytes counted once.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] llama-agent unit/integration tests with the scripted/fake generation seam (<10s, no real model): fork clones history + aliases state; first forked prompt computes strict-prefix offset (no rollback path taken); parent unaffected; unknown-parent fork errors cleanly; byte-accounting test for shared blobs.

 - [ ] `cargo test -p llama-agent` green.
